body:
## Summary

Fixes #714 by making agentic actions capability-driven and by retaining the real work performed by the Agent CLI:

- `Report` selects any advertised run capability and emits a safely quoted, non-interactive `gh issue create` command. The returned issue URL completes the same tool loop.
- ephemeral OpenCode sessions enable its documented Exa-backed `websearch`; search and fetch remain distinct typed capabilities rather than provider prose or client-name branches.
- report language, repository identity, templates, capability aliases, and localized action meanings are seed-backed. Exact whole-action classification prevents “write a report” from causing a side effect.
- completed client tool name/arguments/output are persisted as a durable `tool_call` event, and the final learned task cites that verified evidence. Stable ids merge retries.
- Chat Completions, Responses, Anthropic Messages, and Gemini use the shared completed-exchange recorder. Gemini now translates full `functionCall`/`functionResponse` history and continues after client execution.

Formal AI remains the planner and memory layer; the Agent CLI remains the permission and execution boundary. The implementation generalizes to future tools by advertised capability and transcript shape instead of hard-coded CLI brands.

## Root cause and reproduction

The original screenshot combined two gaps. OpenCode did not advertise `websearch` without `OPENCODE_ENABLE_EXA`, while Formal AI had no seeded report-action recipe. Both therefore fell through to ordinary text.

The deeper review found a third architectural gap: live API memory stored only the prompt and final prose, discarding the real client tool call and result. A minimal regression recovered zero executions from an unnamed OpenAI tool result and recorded only two memory events instead of the required user + tool + final task evidence. Those regressions were written and failed before the shared recorder was implemented.

The requirement matrix, root-cause analysis, protocol research, original screenshot, and before-fix output are preserved in [`docs/case-studies/issue-714/`](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/tree/issue-714-079bdbfda541/docs/case-studies/issue-714).

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `scripts/sync-seed.sh` followed by a clean generated-seed diff
- issue-714 unit regressions: 10 passed
- issue-714 protocol integrations: 4 passed
- full all-feature suite: 1,632 unit tests passed (2 existing ignored), 94 integration tests passed, and 475 library tests passed
- release build + real installed `@link-assistant/agent` E2E: production Formal AI emitted `gh issue create`, the sandboxed client returned `issues/999`, and durable memory retained the command/result in 2 API rounds

The focused real-Agent experiment runs in release CI and cannot mutate GitHub because its fixture places a sandboxed `gh` first on `PATH`.

## Screenshot

Original issue reproduction:

![OpenCode agentic-mode issue reproduction](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-714-079bdbfda541/docs/case-studies/issue-714/raw-data/issue-714-screenshot.png?raw=true)

Fixes #714

title: Generalize agentic tools and retain client execution learning
